analysis_result: Immune cells comprise approximately 10.4% of cells in MPNST-G1 samples overall (2,961 / 28,476 post-QC cells). Per sample: M803 = 19.16% (1,836/9,583), M3048 = 2.87% (195/6,806), M2372 = 7.69% (930/12,087). Immune populations were identified as 4 Leiden clusters (clusters 7, 9, 20: PTPRC+ myeloid/macrophage with CD14/CD68/CD163/ITGAM/CD4 expression; cluster 14: PTPRC+ lymphoid with CD3D/CD3E/CD3G/NKG7/CD4 expression).
denominator_used: N = 28,476 post-QC nuclei from three MPNST-G1 snRNA-seq samples (M803: 9,583; M3048: 6,806; M2372: 12,087). Pre-QC droplets: 6,794,880 per sample (raw 10X barcodes); retained nuclei = those with >=500 genes detected AND <10% mitochondrial reads.
assumptions_made: Used scanpy.read_10x_mtx on per-sample 10X matrices (barcodes/features/matrix.mtx.gz); applied standard QC of >=500 genes and <10% MT reads per cell; merged samples with inner-join on genes (33,694 genes shared); standard normalization (target_sum=1e4) + log1p + 3000 HVGs (Seurat flavor, batch_key=sample) + scale + 50 PCs + neighbors (n=15, 40 PCs) + Leiden (resolution=0.8, seed=42) yielding 25 clusters; classified a cluster as immune if cluster-mean PTPRC log-expression > 0.5 AND >20% of cells PTPRC+; this captured 3 myeloid clusters (7, 9, 20 - strong CD163/CD14/CD68) and one lymphoid cluster (14 - CD3D/CD3E/CD3G/NKG7+); a borderline cluster 11 (PTPRC mean 0.34, 18% fraction pos) without other immune markers was excluded; no integration/batch correction beyond batch-aware HVG selection; no doublet removal; no ambient-RNA decontamination - these may shift per-sample percentages; large between-sample variability (2.87%-19.16%) likely reflects true tumor heterogeneity and/or dissociation/sampling differences; gene symbols were used as var_names (Ensembl->Symbol mapping per the features.tsv).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - GSE207399_10x per-sample 10X Genomics matrices for three MPNST-G1 samples: M803 (GSM6285492), M3048 (GSM6285491), M2372 (GSM6285490).
   - Each loaded via scanpy.read_10x_mtx (barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz [ENSG + gene symbol], matrix.mtx.gz).
   - Raw droplet counts per sample (pre-QC): 6,794,880 barcodes each (unfiltered cellranger output).

B. COHORT DEFINITION (QC)
   - Per-sample filter: n_genes_by_counts >= 500 AND pct_counts_mt < 10.
   - M803: 6,794,880 -> 9,583 nuclei
   - M3048: 6,794,880 -> 6,806 nuclei
   - M2372: 6,794,880 -> 12,087 nuclei
   - Merged (inner-join on 33,694 shared genes): 28,476 nuclei (analytic denominator).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Clustering variable: Leiden (resolution=0.8, seed=42) -> 25 clusters (0..24).
   - Immune markers probed: PTPRC (pan-leukocyte/CD45), CD3D/CD3E/CD3G (T-cell), CD4/CD8A/CD8B (T-subset), CD14/CD68/CD163/LYZ/ITGAM (myeloid/macrophage), MS4A1 (B-cell), NKG7 (NK/CTL). All 14 markers present in the dataset.
   - Immune classification rule: cluster-level mean(log1p PTPRC) > 0.5 AND cluster-level fraction PTPRC>0 > 0.20.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Pipeline: per-sample QC -> merge -> normalize_total(1e4) -> log1p -> highly_variable_genes(n=3000, Seurat, batch_key=sample) -> scale -> PCA(50 comps) -> neighbors(n=15, n_pcs=40) -> Leiden(res=0.8).
   - Clusters meeting immune rule: 7 (mean PTPRC=1.32, 59% PTPRC+; CD163 35%, CD14 14%, ITGAM 19%, CD4 15% - macrophage/myeloid), 9 (mean PTPRC=1.23, 58% PTPRC+; CD163 87%, ITGAM 31%, CD4 17% - macrophage), 14 (mean PTPRC=2.64, 86% PTPRC+; CD3G 8%, CD3E 4.4%, CD3D 3%, NKG7 3.7%, CD4 8% - T/NK lymphoid), 20 (mean PTPRC=2.32, 98% PTPRC+; CD163 99%, ITGAM 69%, CD14 30%, CD68 15%, CD4 58% - mature macrophage).
   - Immune cells = union of clusters 7, 9, 14, 20: n = 2,961.
   - Overall proportion = 2,961 / 28,476 = 10.40%.

E. RESULT
   - OVERALL: 10.40% immune (2,961 / 28,476).
   - M803: 19.16% (1,836 / 9,583)
   - M3048: 2.87% (195 / 6,806)
   - M2372: 7.69% (930 / 12,087)

F. CAVEATS
   - snRNA-seq has sparse per-cell detection for low-expressing immune markers like CD3D/CD3E; we therefore used cluster-level mean expression + fraction-expressing thresholds on PTPRC (pan-leukocyte) rather than requiring individual-cell positivity for multiple markers.
   - No batch-correction (e.g., Harmony/scVI) was applied beyond batch-aware HVG selection; per-sample composition differences may partly reflect batch/dissociation effects, though the magnitude (M803 ~19% vs M3048 ~3%) is large and likely also reflects true tumor heterogeneity.
   - No doublet removal (scrublet) nor ambient RNA decontamination (SoupX/CellBender) was applied; these could modestly affect proportions but are unlikely to change the order-of-magnitude result.
   - Cluster 11 (PTPRC mean 0.34, 18% PTPRC+) did not meet the immune threshold and was treated as non-immune; moving this borderline cluster would change the overall proportion by ~1-2 percentage points.
   - Gene symbols used as var_names (uniquified); analysis was done in-memory with anndata.
